questions.question: What is the key behavioural distinction between `computed` and `watch`?
options: `computed` derives a cached value; `watch` runs side effects in response to changes
`computed` can watch multiple sources; `watch` only tracks one
`computed` re-runs on every render; `watch` only fires on mount

questions.question: Which of the following is a sign that you should replace a `watch` with a `computed`?
options: The watcher performs an async API call when a value changes
The watcher sets a `ref` to a value derived from another reactive source
The watcher needs access to both the previous and new values
The watcher writes to `localStorage` when the user changes a setting

questions.question: What does `watch` provide that `computed` cannot?
options: Automatic caching of the derived value between renders
Access to both the new value and the previous value
The ability to depend on multiple reactive sources at once
A synchronous way to derive a value for use in templates
